title: quercus prinus var. bicolor
intro: Quercus prinus var. bicolor, also known as the Bicolor Oak or Swamp White Oak, is a variant of the White Oak tree (Quercus alba) characterized by its distinctive bicolor foliage. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Quercus prinus var. bicolor include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and support digestive health. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and can also be used in tinctures or salves to harness its medicinal properties.
